situational_context: Extended low-training-load period: chronic load at 17.8 (~1.1% of 2023 historical peak of 1608). Activity in 2026 limited to Zwift VirtualRides (8 sessions, ~30 min each) and 2 outdoor rides — zero running since late 2025. HRV 7d avg 38 ms (was 42 at bootstrap). RHR 52-53 bpm stable. Sleep avg 7.55h. Weight trending down: 107→105.22 kg over 5 weeks (-0.36 kg/wk, ahead of target rate for 95 kg goal). Body recomp signal: fat% declining (28→26.6%) while muscle mass INCREASING (71→73.3 kg) despite low protein. Infant cohabitant constrains training windows — morning/lunchtime slots most viable. CRITICAL NUTRITIONAL DEFICIT: protein 56g/day vs 160g target (35% of target). This is below the 1.0g/kg lean mass preservation floor and risks undermining all performance objectives. ATHLETE PROFILE: historically competitive recreational endurance athlete (PR semi 1:34:33, PR marathon 3:39:55 at ~87 kg in 2019, top 12-15% French amateur); currently in advanced detraining after paternity-driven volume collapse. All physiological rebuilding capacity is intact — RHR and VO2max holdover confirms residual cardiac adaptation.

recommendations.text: Fix protein intake immediately: target ≥120g/day this week, build to 160g/day over 4 weeks. Practical path: +2 scoops whey isolate (+50g) + 200g Greek yogurt (+20g) + protein-dense lunch = +70-80g added to current 56g base.
recommendations.measurable: 30d avg protein ≥100g/day by May 27, ≥130g/day by June 27

recommendations.text: Begin running immediately: 3x/week easy runs (Z1-Z2, ≤30 min), starting this week. No hero sessions. Consistent frequency > intensity.
recommendations.measurable: ≥3 run sessions logged in the 7-day window ending May 4

recommendations.text: Schedule GP appointment for annual blood panel: NFS, lipids, ferritin, vitamin D, TSH, testosterone.

markers_to_watch.reason: Currently 56g/day — below 1.0g/kg preservation floor; risks lean mass catabolism during weight loss and blunts all training adaptations
